text.content:
“Ahh, hello. Have you come for goods? Or perhaps... to relieve yourself of souls?”

The hunched Handmaid trades in banked souls (spend from the Bonfire Ledger). Her stock grows as the party fells bosses — she senses great souls and lays out greater wares. Track the Shop Tier (0–3) on the Bonfire Ledger’s secondary resource bar; raise it by one each time a boss or mini-boss falls.

Shop Tier ladder

TierReached when…
0You first reach Firelink (after Iudex Gundyr). The base shop opens.
1The first High Wall mini-boss falls (Outrider or Pus of Man).
2The second High Wall mini-boss falls.
3The Dragon is bested or you turn toward Vordt — the final loadout.

The shelf (cumulative — each Tier keeps everything below it)

ItemSoulsFromOn hand (grows by Tier)
Firebomb50 eaT0×5, then a fresh ×5 at every Tier
Titanite Shard200 eaT0×2 → +3 @T1 → +3 @T2 → full restock @T3
Estus Shard1,000T0×1, then +1 at each of T1 / T2 / T3 (Fire Keeper adds a charge, cap 6)
Catalyst (Staff / Talisman)1,500T0×1, restocks @T2
Ember600T0×1 → ×2 @T1 → ×2 @T2
Titanite Chunk800 eaT1×1 @T1 → ×1 @T2 → ×2 @T3
Twinkling Titanite2,000T3×1 — rare; for ascending the Pyromancy Flame or a unique blade

Cumulative means a Tier-2 Handmaid still sells everything from Tiers 0–1, just topped back up. Don’t sweat exact counts — the point is the shelf visibly grows as they win. Titanite stays the backstop; finding it on the High Wall is still the cheaper path.

Haggling & intimidation (freewheel it)

Players will try to talk her down or lean on her — say yes and enjoy the scene. Just keep the mechanical lever light so the souls economy holds: a strong Persuasion/Intimidation check earns a one-time ~15% discount on a single purchase, or a small freebie (a Firebomb or two). Never a standing discount, and never a free Estus Shard, Titanite, or catalyst — those are the spine of the difficulty curve. She’s a non-combatant in the one safe hub: escalate to threats and she turns cold and cryptic (or charges more) — but Firelink never rolls initiative.

Selling

The Handmaid buys nothing useful back — souls are the only currency that matters. (If a player insists on selling loot, value it at the table; it isn’t part of the intended economy.)

Optional drift: roll the Shrine Handmaid — Restock table between visits to vary what’s freshly on the shelf.

“Need something reinforced? You’ve come to the right place. Titanite, and I’ll see to the rest.”

Andre reinforces weapons and catalysts with titanite + souls. Each +1 adds +1 attack and +1 damage; the one-shot cap is +3. (Full grid and the scaling system are in the Weapon Scaling & Upgrades handbook.)

Reinforcement (always available)

Reinforce toCost (cumulative)Effect
+11 Titanite Shard + 200 souls+1 attack / +1 damage
+22 Titanite Shards + 400 souls+2 attack / +2 damage
+33 Titanite Shards (or 1 Titanite Chunk) + 600 souls+3 attack / +3 damage

On a catalyst, the +N adds to spell attack rolls and spell damage instead.

Andre’s titanite (grows with the party’s deeds)

Finding titanite is the intended path, but Andre keeps a small supply so a determined upgrader is never hard-blocked. What he carries — and the services he’ll offer — rise with the Shop Tier:

TierAndre stocksNew service
0Titanite Shard ×2 (200 ea)Reinforce +1–+3; stoke the Pyromancy Flame
1Titanite Shard ×3 (200 ea)—
2Titanite Chunk ×1 (800)Estus +1 (2d4+2 → 2d4+4) if the Fire Keeper kindles it
3Titanite Chunk ×1 (800)Pyromancy Flame ascension (1 Twinkling Titanite + 600 souls): +1 spell damage to pyromancies. Estus +2 (2d4+4 → 2d6+4)

Estus reinforcement (the Fire Keeper’s domain)

Andre strengthens the Estus Flask itself once the Fire Keeper kindles it: each Estus +N raises healing one step (2d4+2 → 2d4+4 → 2d6+4). Optional for a one-shot — offer it only if the party banks a surplus (Tier 2+).

DM: applying an upgrade in Foundry

Bump the weapon’s flags.ashen.upgrade and add the same +N to the attack activity’s attack bonus and damage bonus. Or simply narrate it and have the player add the flat number by hand — the math is identical, and the weapon card’s Upgrade line is just a reminder.

"Welcome to Firelink Shrine, Unkindled One. I shall stay by your side... until the day you become Cinder."

The Fire Keeper spends banked souls to raise levels. The full procedure and the per-level choice menu live in the Level-Up Menu Cards journal; run the Level Up macro to spend souls from the Bonfire Ledger and announce the new level.

Quick reminder of the cost curve: 3→4 500 · 4→5 800 · 5→6 1,200 · 6→7 1,800 · 7→8 2,600 · 8→9 3,600 · 9→10 5,000.
